---
description: Markdown writing and documentation standards
applyTo: "**/*.md"
---

# Markdown Documentation Standards

When creating or editing Markdown documentation:

- Use ATX-style headings (`#`) with a single H1 per document
- Write in active voice with clear, concise sentences
- Use sentence case for headings (capitalise only the first word)
- Add alt text to all images for accessibility
- Use fenced code blocks with language identifiers for syntax highlighting
- Prefer reference-style links for URLs used multiple times
- Keep lines under 120 characters where practical
- Use ordered lists only when sequence matters; use unordered lists otherwise
- Include a blank line before and after headings, lists, and code blocks
